zh.lifeSummary: 當John De Farrington於1270年出生在英國藍卡爾沃爾登 霍爾時，他的父親William De Farrington26歲，母親Jane De Redmayne22歲。他於大約1297年與Clarissa Kighley在英國英格蘭結婚。他們至少是3個兒子的父母。他去世在英國藍卡爾法利頓。

nameMeanings.name: Farrington
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Farrington in Lancashire, but perhaps occasionally from Farrington Gurney in Somerset. Both are named from Old English fearn ‘fern’ + tūn ‘settlement’. The surname also probably reached America via Ireland, where it is recorded as early as the 14th century.

variants.name: Fearrington
meanings: Perhaps an altered form of   Farrington  .

nameMeanings.name: John
meanings: Greek: the Lord gave graciously; whom Jehovah gave; whom Jehovah bestowed.

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When John De Farrington was born in 1270, in Worden Hall, Lancashire, England, his father, William De Farrington, was 26 and his mother, Jane De Redmayne, was 22. He married Clarissa Kighley about 1297, in England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons. He died in Farington, Lancashire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque John De Farrington est né en 1270, à Worden Hall, Lancashire, Angleterre, son père, William De Farrington, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Jane De Redmayne, avait 22 ans. Il a épousé Clarissa Kighley environ 1297, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils. Il est décédé à Farington, Lancashire, Angleterre.

ja.lifeSummary: John De Farringtonが1270年にイギリスランカシュアイアウオルデン ハルで生まれた時，父William De Farringtonは26歳，母Jane De Redmayneは22歳であった。約1297年にイギリスイギリスでClarissa Kighleyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子の親であった。彼はイギリスランカシュアイアファリントンで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: John De Farrington(이)가 1270년 영국 랭카실르 워든 홀에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William De Farrington(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Jane De Redmayne(은)는 22세였다. 그는 대략 1297년 영국 잉글랜드에서 Clarissa Kighley(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 영국 랭카실르 패링턴에서 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда John De Farrington родился в 1270 г. в местности ворден холл, ланкашир, Англия, его отцу по имени William De Farrington было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Jane De Redmayne, 22. Он женился на женщине по имени Clarissa Kighley около 1297 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына. Он умер в местности фарингтон, ланкашир, Англия.
